uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds fix. bytes[0]=0x30 (type 3). bytes 1-4 / 5-8 are big-endian IEEE-754 float32 encodings of longitude -122.25 and latitude 37.5; bytes 9-12 are big-endian uint32 UTC epoch seconds. expected.data derived by decoding the IEEE-754 floats correctly (the upstream hex2float uses a 0x7fffff mantissa divisor and is off by ~6e-6; we use 0x800000) and remapping to the normalized vocabulary.

uplink.description: Heartbeat (0x2): battery 85%, moving, GPS located, charging
uplink._note: Synthetic heartbeat. bytes[0]=0x20 (type 2). bytes[1]=battery percent, bytes[2]=RSSI magnitude (negated), bytes[3..4]=SNR*0.01 big-endian (0x0258=600 -> 6.0 dB), bytes[5] high nibble=GPS state 3 (located) / low nibble=move state 1 (moving), bytes[6] high nibble=charge state 5 (charging). expected.data remapped to the vocabulary: move state -> action.motion.detected, percent -> batteryPercent.

uplink.description: Beacon (0x7): one BLE beacon scan, cloud-solved (no on-device fix)
uplink._note: Synthetic beacon frame. bytes[0]=0x71 (type 7, low nibble = beacon count 1). bytes[1]=movement level. Each beacon tuple starts at offset 6: major bytes[6..7]=0x1234, minor bytes[8..9]=0x5678, rssi byte bytes[10]=200 -> 200-256=-56 dBm. BLE positioning is solved off-device, so no position.* is synthesized; the scan list is emitted as the bleBeacons extra.
Synthetic alarm frame. bytes[0]=0x80 (type 8), bytes[1]=1 -> SOS alarm. expected.data emits the alarm string as a camelCase extra.

uplink.description: Register (0x1) carries configuration, not a measurement
uplink._note: Synthetic register frame. bytes[0]=0x10 (type 1). The register frame is device configuration (ADR, DR, periods, firmware version) with no normalized measurement, so an error is returned.

uplink.description: Unsupported message type yields an error
uplink._note: Synthetic error vector. bytes[0]=0x40 (type 4, not defined for this device).
